title: Pushbullet API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Pushbullet API Key detected. Pushbullet is a notification and messaging service that enables cross-device communication, push notifications, and file sharing. API keys (access tokens) provide full programmatic access to your Pushbullet account including sending notifications to all devices, accessing message history, managing devices, sharing files, and controlling SMS/MMS functionality. Exposing these keys enables attackers to send spam notifications to your devices, read your message history, access shared files, impersonate you to send messages, monitor your device activity, and potentially access sensitive personal information. Revoke this key immediately from https://www.pushbullet.com/#settings/account, generate a new API key, rotate any related credentials, audit recent notification and message activity for unauthorized access, implement environment-based token storage, and use restricted API keys with minimal required permissions where possible.

Pushbullet API Key detected in variable assignment. Pushbullet is a notification and messaging platform enabling cross-device communication. This API key (access token) grants complete access to your Pushbullet account including sending push notifications, reading message history, managing connected devices, sharing files, sending SMS/MMS messages, and accessing personal data. Exposing this key allows attackers to spam your devices with notifications, read your private messages, access shared files, send messages impersonating you, monitor your activity patterns, and extract sensitive information from your communication history. Immediately revoke this key from your Pushbullet account settings at https://www.pushbullet.com/#settings/account, generate a replacement key, implement secure environment-based credential storage, audit recent activity logs for unauthorized access, review all sent notifications and messages, and use granular permissions with separate keys for different applications where possible.
